summary: Gruber defends Apple's widely mocked decision to place the Magic Mouse charging port on the bottom, arguing it's a deliberate design trade-off rather than an oversight. He contends Apple prioritizes visual symmetry and the wireless-only experience, noting that a front-facing port would compromise the mouse's sleek appearance. He compares it to Apple's historical commitment to the single-button mouse and argues that the 9-year unchanged design proves Apple sees it as a feature, not a mistake. He acknowledges the inconvenience but notes it only affects users every few months, with battery warnings provided. In a disarming twist, Gruber admits he doesn't actually use the Magic Mouse himself, preferring a cheap Lenovo mouse for comfort and customization.
key_points: Apple sees the bottom charging port as a trade-off worth making for visual symmetry and an uninterrupted top surface that slopes to the desk
The inability to use the mouse while charging may be intentional — Apple wants a purely wireless experience without cable tension affecting the feel
The 9-year unchanged design without even attempted tweaks (unlike the butterfly keyboard) suggests Apple considers this a successful design choice, not a mistake
The vocal critics are a small minority; Gruber suspects the Magic Mouse has high customer satisfaction overall
Gruber transparently undermines his own authority by admitting he hasn't used an Apple mouse since the 1990s and his daily driver is a cheap Lenovo mouse
money_quotes: Apple obviously just sees this as a trade-off worth making. Apple wants the mouse to be visually symmetric, and they want the top surface to slope all the way down to the desk or table top it rests upon.
My other hunch is that the Magic Mouse's designers actually see the inability to use it while plugged in as a feature, not a bug.
I know for a fact that Apple designers have considered designs for a mouse with the port exposed at the front, and everything they came up with looked worse.
With this design, the mouse looks better 100 percent of the time it's in use, and it looks a bit silly every few months when you need to charge it.
That 5-year stretch shows that while Apple doesn't necessarily fix design mistakes quickly, they attempt to fix them quickly.
The Magic Mouse charging port placement is an opinionated design, not an absurd design.
Apple sticking with this design in the face of vociferous peanut gallery mockery reminds me of the company's noble commitment to a single-button mouse.
No joke, my daily driver for the last 4 years has been this simple Lenovo mouse I bought on a lark back in December 2020.
key_insight: The Magic Mouse's bottom charging port is a deliberate, opinionated design choice that prioritizes form and wireless purity over charging convenience — and Apple's refusal to change it for nine years proves they see it as a feature, not a flaw.
